items.title: 🔴 #1 — Update Deck: Innovate South Presentation
items.body: DO THIS FIRST. Update the Innovate South presentation deck before anything else today. Public-facing pitch — highest leverage task of the day.

items.title: 🔴 #2 — Build Stability.org Deck
items.body: Build or update the internal Stability.org funding deck. This feeds directly into all grant applications and funding conversations. High priority — do before grant outreach.

items.title: 🟠 #3 — Schedule AI Meetup Event
items.body: Get the new AI meetup event scheduled and on the calendar. High priority.

items.title: 🟡 #4 — FEMA BRIC Grant → Ask Becky
items.body:
FEMA Building Resilient Infrastructure and Communities (BRIC) — $1 billion pool, national program. Strong fit for Stability.org (infrastructure resilience, community preparedness, disaster mitigation). Forward to Becky to apply.

URL: https://www.fema.gov/press-release/20260325/fema-announces-1-billion-federal-funding-help-states-mitigate-impact

Key points:
• Prioritizes shovel-ready infrastructure projects
• Simplified application process (fewer bureaucratic hurdles)
• States, local governments, territories, Tribal Nations eligible

items.title: 🟡 #5 — Duke Energy Foundation Grant → Ask Becky
items.body:
Duke Energy Foundation storm preparedness grants — up to $25,000 per org. Deadline: April 30, 2026. North Carolina orgs only — confirm Stability.org has NC reach before applying.

Apply: https://foundation.duke-energy.com/grants/north-carolina

Funding supports: community preparedness training, emergency planning, local response collaboration, public education for severe weather readiness.

items.title: 🟡 #6 — SBIR Grant Applications → Ask Becky
items.body:
Forward Becky the Hutch email — she runs with SBIR applications for Stability.org.

Target agencies (priority order):
• NSF — AI-driven civic resilience, novel tech angle
• DHS / Homeland Security — emergency management, community preparedness
• DoD (Army) — disaster response, comms infrastructure
• USDA — rural community angle (strong Louisiana fit)

Key steps for Becky:
• Review SBIR.gov for recent awards in target categories
• Contact Jerry Hollister @ BBC Consulting for application support
• Complete APEX fiscal readiness assessment before submitting
• Hutch seminar (Baton Rouge) focused on NASA + DoD SBIR — confirm if she can attend

Note: Keep Stability.org separate from TextOS/Reyla (nonprofit vs. commercial).

items.title: 🔴 #7 — Ground Force Financials → Jack (CPA)
items.body:
Get Ground Force Humanitarian Aid financial documents to Jack the CPA. Likely needs:
• Bank statements (download from bank portal — PDF or CSV, last 12 months)
• QuickBooks export — run P&L + Balance Sheet, export as PDF or Excel

Text Jack first: 'Hey Jack, what do you need from us for Ground Force? Bank statements? QuickBooks export?' — confirm before pulling everything.

items.title: 💵 Contact Greg Acres — Deposit Refund
items.body: Reach out to Greg Acres and ask about getting your deposit money back.

items.title: 💰 File Personal Taxes
items.body: Get your personal taxes done. Deadline is April 15.

items.title: 🧾 Get Tax Filing & Bank Statements for Jack (CPA)
items.body: Gather tax filing information and bank statements and get them over to Jack, your CPA.

items.title: 🦞 OpenClaw — Social Media Sharing Agents
items.body: Next priority for OpenClaw: get the social media sharing agents working. Goal is automated or one-tap sharing of content across platforms via agent workflows.

items.title: 🤖 Reyla — Paywall + PDF Brochure Generator
items.body:
Two things to tackle for Reyla:
1. Build the paywall
2. One-click PDF brochure generation — user clicks, gets a polished brochure PDF

items.title: 📱 TextOS — Paywall
items.body: Time to build the TextOS paywall. Monetization priority.

items.title: ✅ Write Greg's Recommendation
items.body: Don't forget to fill out the recommendation for Greg.

items.title: Chelsea's Anthropic courses — review when you have 45 mins
items.body:
Chelsea asked you to complete 4 Anthropic Skilljar courses for her partner certification. Links:

1. Introduction to Agent Skills → https://anthropic.skilljar.com/introduction-to-agent-skills
2. Claude with the Anthropic API → https://anthropic.skilljar.com/claude-with-the-anthropic-api
3. Introduction to Model Context Protocol → https://anthropic.skilljar.com/introduction-to-model-context-protocol
4. Claude Code in Action → https://anthropic.skilljar.com/claude-code-in-action

items.title: InkThorn Playbook is live!
items.body: Text notes to your Rob-Agent on Telegram and they'll appear here automatically. Your agents will also publish daily briefings to this panel.
